UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the Paisano Sales Hub application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

firebaseUid.description: The Firebase Authentication UID associated with the user.

role.description: The user's role (Manager, Seller). Manager has administrative privileges.

avatarUrl.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

Lead.title: Lead
Lead.description: Represents a lead or prospect in the sales process.

sellerId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Lead) The ID of the seller assigned to the lead.

sellerName.description: Denormalized name of the seller assigned to the lead for efficient display.

contactPerson.description: Name of the contact person at the client company.

secondContactName.description: Name of the secondary contact person.

secondContactPhone.description: Phone number of the secondary contact person.

clientType.description: Type of the client (e.g., Dealer, EMPRESA DE TRANSPORTE, Sand Industry, USUARIO FINAL).

status.description: Status of the lead (e.g., New, Contacted, Qualified).

tag.description: A descriptive tag for quick visual identification in the UI (e.g., danger, warning, success).

leadId.description: Reference to Lead. (Relationship: Lead 1:N Opportunity) The ID of the associated lead.

sellerId.description: Denormalized seller ID for rules and queries.

stage.description: The current stage of the opportunity in the sales pipeline.

value.description: The monetary value of the opportunity.

currency.description: The currency of the opportunity value.

probability.description: The probability of closing the opportunity (0-100).

expectedCloseDate.description: The expected date to close the opportunity.

sentPrices.description: Indicates if prices were sent as part of moving to the 'Information Sent' stage.

sentTechnicalInfo.description: Indicates if technical information was sent as part of moving to the 'Information Sent' stage.

sentCompanyInfo.description: Indicates if company information was sent as part of moving to the 'Information Sent' stage.

sentMedia.description: Indicates if photos or videos were sent as part of moving to the 'Information Sent' stage.

infoSentContactChannels.description: The contact channels used for the contact when information was sent.

infoSentNotes.description: Observations from the information sent stage.

acceptedPrice.description: Indicates if the client accepted the quoted price.

requestsDiscount.description: Indicates if the client requested a discount.

negotiationDate.description: Date when the negotiation stage started.

agreedDeliveryTime.description: The agreed delivery time in weeks, set during negotiation.

clientMadeDownPayment.description: Indicates if the client has made the advance payment for the sale.

deliveryTimeConfirmed.description: Indicates if the delivery time has been confirmed with the client upon closing.

closingNotes.description: Optional observations recorded at the closing stage.

closingDate.description: Date when the sale was closed.

financiamientoExternoNotes.description: Observations from the external financing stage.

financiamientoExternoDate.description: Date when the opportunity was moved to external financing.

discardedDate.description: Date when the opportunity was discarded.

discardReason.description: Reason why the opportunity was discarded.

Quotation.title: Quotation
Quotation.description: Represents a quotation for a sales opportunity.

opportunityId.description: Reference to Opportunity. (Relationship: Opportunity 1:N Quotation) The ID of the associated opportunity.

sellerId.description: Denormalized seller ID for rules and queries.

pdfUrl.description: URL of the quotation PDF stored in Firebase Storage.

value.description: The total value of the quotation.

currency.description: The currency of the quotation value.

version.description: The version number of the quotation.

Activity.title: Activity
Activity.description: Represents an activity related to a lead or opportunity.

leadId.description: Reference to Lead. (Relationship: Lead 1:N Activity) The ID of the associated lead.

quotationId.description: Optional reference to a Quotation. Indicates this activity is a follow-up for a specific quotation.

sellerId.description: Denormalized seller ID for rules and queries.

type.description: Type of activity (e.g., Call, Email, Meeting).

contactChannels.description: The channels used for the contact (e.g., WhatsApp, Messenger, Call).

completed.description: Indicates if the activity is completed.

clientResponded.description: Indicates if the client responded during this activity.

completionNotes.description: Notes recorded upon completing the activity.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product or service offered.

sellerId.description: The ID of the seller who created the product.

sellerName.description: Denormalized name of the seller for display.

summary.description: AI-generated summary of the product description, for use in quotations.

currency.description: The currency of the product price.

Template.title: Template
Template.description: Represents a message template for emails, WhatsApp, etc.

sellerId.description: The ID of the seller who created the template.

sellerName.description: Denormalized name of the seller for display.

subject.description: The subject line for email templates.

content.description: The body/content of the message template.

type.description: Type of template (e.g., Email, WhatsApp, SMS).

AppSettings.title: AppSettings
AppSettings.description: Stores global settings for the application.

logoUrl.description: URL of the application's global logo.

definition.description: Stores a user's profile document. The document ID {userId} matches the Firebase Auth UID. Profile information contains the user's role, enabling role-based access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores lead information. Includes the denormalized 'sellerId' to enable efficient querying of leads owned by a specific seller. Managers can access all leads.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lead.

definition.description: Stores sales opportunity information. References the associated lead via 'leadId'.

params.name: opportunityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the opportunity.

definition.description: Stores quotation information. References the associated opportunity via 'opportunityId'.

params.name: quotationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the quotation.

definition.description: Stores activity information related to leads. References the associated lead via 'leadId'.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier of the activity.

definition.description: Stores product information. Each product is owned by a seller.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores user-created message templates. Each template is owned by a seller.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier of the template.

definition.description: Stores global application settings. For example, `settings/app` could hold the logo URL.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The ID of the settings document (e.g., 'app').

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to facilitate a secure and scalable Paisano Sales Hub application, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). It leverages Structural Segregation, Access Modeling, and Data Clarity to ensure maintainability and security.

**Authorization Independence:** Denormalization is heavily used to ensure authorization rules do not rely on `get()` calls. For example, the `UserProfile`'s role is not determined by looking up another document, but instead stored directly within the `UserProfile` document. Similarly, for leads, the `sellerId` is denormalized, which is crucial for read operations and avoids complex rule evaluations.

**Structural Segregation:**  The structure separates user profiles from other data, storing them in `/users/{userId}/profile`. Leads are stored in the `/leads/{leadId}` collection and include the denormalized `sellerId`, enabling efficient querying of leads owned by a specific seller and simplifying security rules.

**Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is implemented for user profiles (`/users/{userId}/profile`). Leads and other collaborative data leverage denormalized fields (`sellerId` for leads) to simplify access control.

**QAPs Support:** The structure enables secure `list` operations. Because the `sellerId` is denormalized into the `Lead` document, rules can efficiently filter and authorize reads based on the requesting user's ID without performing costly `get()` operations. Similarly, managers can list all leads due to their roles.

**Invariants:** The structure supports the integrity of data relationships through explicit field references (`leadId` in `Opportunity`, `opportunityId` in `Quotation`). Timestamps (`createdDate`) are included to enable ordering and audit trails.
